The AI meeting-assistant market has matured rapidly through 2024–2025, with tools now offering real-time transcription, speaker diarization, automated action-item extraction, and integration with major video-conferencing platforms. For a small university research lab (12 members, budget-sensitive, using Zoom and Google Meet), the key considerations are: (a) per-seat pricing vs. team plans, (b) support for both Zoom and Google Meet, (c) searchable transcript storage, (d) speaker identification accuracy, (e) export capabilities, and (f) data privacy, consent, and retention policies—especially critical in an academic research context where confidentiality of discussions may be required.
